title: Bulk report - Week 5
summary:
Capesize
Whilst the Chinese New Year was celebrated there was a fall in all rates this week with west Australia/China route now trading sub $5.00 with a rumour of perhaps $4.85 being fixed by Rio Tinto today. There was very little timecharter enquiry or fixing, the Anangel Grace (180,391 built 2010) fixed retro sailing Liuheng 31 January for an Australian round voyage at $7,000 mid-week; currently brokers assess the route around $6,000 daily. There was some activity for Saldahna Bay to Qingdao with Sedibang Iron Ore fixing tonnage basis 160,000 min/max for 15/20 February at $8.70.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Whilst the Chinese New Year was celebrated there was a fall in all rates this week with west Australia/China route now trading sub $5.00 with a rumour of perhaps $4.85 being fixed by Rio Tinto today. There was very little timecharter enquiry or fixing, the Anangel Grace (180,391 built 2010) fixed retro sailing Liuheng 31 January for an Australian round voyage at $7,000 mid-week; currently brokers assess the route around $6,000 daily.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: With the holidays in the East the early part of the week the market started out relatively flat and directionless. However with the return to work for many market participants, enquiry in both basins has picked up including some period cover being taken.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: Some Asian countries came back to work from Tuesday and China resumed from today, which limited the activity. On period front, a 55,000-dwt 2004-built delivery in Canakkale was fixed for a minimum of 76 days up to a maximum of 140 days with worldwide delivery at $10,550 daily.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The year of the Rooster struts it way in. The Stones once sung about ‘A little red rooster ‘ but this week the song on owners' minds is more likely to be ‘I can't get no satisfaction'.
